settings.label: Execution Mode

options.label: System Killer (unrestricted — danger)
options.confirm: System Killer mode gives Claude Code FULL unrestricted access including shell, package installs, and system commands. Are you sure?

settings.help: Controls what Claude Code is allowed to do

settings.label: Workspace Directory
settings.help: Where Claude Code creates project directories (outside Sapphire)

settings.label: Max Turns Per Session
settings.help: Limits how many iterations Claude Code can take per session

settings.label: Base Instructions (all sessions)
settings.default:
You are a code builder working for Sapphire AI.
- Write clean, working code
- Test your work by running it before reporting done
- When possible, make unit tests or regression tests to verify your code
- Keep it simple and minimal — no over-engineering
- If you hit a problem you can't solve, describe it clearly in your final response
- If you notice anything noteworthy, write it to NOTES.md
- Sapphire is alive and running while you work. NEVER restart her, NEVER run systemctl, NEVER kill her process. She is not your test environment — she is who you are building for. Write the code and report back.
settings.help: Base layer — included in EVERY Claude Code session (project and plugin). Keep universal.

settings.label: Project Instructions
settings.default:
- Include a README.md with usage instructions
- Keep dependencies minimal
- For web apps: build as a self-contained index.html with inline JS/CSS (no build step, no npm)
- Do not install system-wide packages
settings.help: Added to CLAUDE.md for general project builds (~/claude-workspaces/). Not included in plugin builds.

settings.label: Plugin Instructions
settings.default:
- DO NOT ASK FOR CONFIRMATION — build the plugin immediately, do not plan and wait
- NEVER restart Sapphire — do not run systemctl, do not run restart-sapphire.sh, do not kill processes. Your job is to write code only.
- Build ALL files at the ROOT of this workspace directory
- plugin.json goes at workspace root, tools/ folder at workspace root
- Do NOT create user/plugins/ or any subdirectory structure — this workspace IS the plugin directory
- capabilities.tools MUST be a JSON array: ["tools/name_tools.py"] — NOT a string
- capabilities.hooks is a dict: {"pre_chat": "hooks/handler.py"}
- Include 'author' field in plugin.json
- No import restrictions — use whatever the plugin needs (subprocess, os, etc.)
- Reference: docs/plugin-author/examples.md has working templates for every plugin type
- Reference: plugins/elevenlabs/ is a real working TTS provider plugin
- You have READ access to user/logs/sapphire.log — check it if told something failed at runtime
settings.help: Added to CLAUDE.md for plugin builds (user/plugins/). Not included in project builds.

settings.label: Plugin Build Model
settings.help: Model for plugin builder sessions (e.g. 'sonnet', 'haiku'). Empty = use default model.

settings.label: Budget Cap (USD)
settings.help: Maximum dollar amount per Claude Code session. Prevents runaway costs on autonomous builds.
